pattern: For each pivot point, group all other points by their squared distance to the pivot; for each group of size k there are k*(k-1) ordered boomerang pairs.
hints: For a fixed pivot i, the boomerang (i,j,k) requires dist(i,j)==dist(i,k); count how many pairs j,k share the same distance from i.
Use a hash map keyed by squared distance (avoids floating point) to bucket all other points for each pivot; the number of ordered pairs from a bucket of size m is m*(m-1).
Iterate over every point as the pivot, rebuild the distance-count map each time, accumulate m*(m-1) for every bucket, then move to the next pivot.

edge_inputs.description: three collinear equidistant points — pivot is the center, giving 2 boomerangs
three points not equidistant from any center — 2 boomerangs
two points — can never form a triple
all four points equidistant from the origin — pivot at origin gives 4*3=12 boomerangs
multiple groups with different distances from a pivot — each group counted independently
points with large coordinates — squared distance must not overflow int32

stress.description: random distinct points within coordinate bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    seen = set()
    pts = []
    while len(pts) < n:
        x = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
        y = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
        if (x, y) not in seen:
            seen.add((x, y))
            pts.append([x, y])
    return [pts]
